config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, penyalahgunaan kekuasaan jadi sangat mudah terjadi. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From my American perspective, that kind of blanket priority is dangerous because history shows how easily governments and majorities can abuse it in the name of the "greater good." I do think individual rights come with responsibilities, and there are real cases where public safety or public health justify limits, but those limits have to be narrow, justified, and accountable. A society is strongest when it protects people precisely when their rights are inconvenient to the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas mutlak di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya berlebihan, dan itu berbahaya karena bisa menutup ruang kritik serta melindungi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas, karena masyarakat yang sehat tidak lahir dari pengorbanan individu yang terus-menerus, melainkan dari aturan yang adil bagi semua. Dalam keadaan tertentu seperti darurat kesehatan atau keamanan, pembatasan bisa saja perlu, tetapi harus proporsional, transparan, dan tidak boleh menjadi dalih untuk menekan kebebasan secara permanen.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea is exactly how majorities and governments start treating rights as optional whenever it is politically convenient, and I am not comfortable with that at all. That said, I do think there are limited cases like public health, emergency response, and basic safety where society can justify narrow restrictions, but only if they are temporary, proportionate, and open to challenge. The burden should always be on society to prove why a limit is necessary, not on the individual to prove why their rights deserve to exist.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus berada di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari kacamata saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menormalisasi pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan itu justru membuka pintu bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kalau ada keadaan darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, pembatasan memang bisa dibenarkan, tetapi harus sangat terbatas, transparan, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan kebiasaan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak warga sambil tetap menjaga kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should never become a blanket excuse to override individual rights, and I am firmly against that idea as a general rule. From my American perspective, the danger is obvious: once you let “the common good” outrank rights by default, it becomes far too easy for governments or majorities to silence dissent, justify overreach, and call it responsibility. I do think limited restrictions can be legitimate in real emergencies like public health or immediate safety, but they have to be narrow, temporary, and openly challengeable. A healthy society protects the individual first, because that is what keeps power from turning abusive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
